skill.name: create-skill-file
skill.description: Guides AI assistants through creating clear SKILL.md files with strong names, descriptions, structure, examples, and review checklists.
skill.summary: Create structured SKILL.md files with templates, examples, and quality checks.
skill.author: YYH211

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many command, URL, environment, and weak-crypto patterns, but manual review shows they are documentation examples and templates. No prompt injection, executable script, credential exfiltration, or malicious runtime behavior was found. Residual risk is low because generated skills may include commands that users should review before execution.

low_findings.title: False positive: Markdown command examples
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported many external command hits, but the reviewed locations are fenced Markdown examples and templates. They document possible skill content and do not execute commands from this package.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are inside Markdown code fences or template placeholders. No executable script file or automatic invocation path was found.

low_findings.title: False positive: Documentation URLs
low_findings.description: Hardcoded URL findings are documentation links, localhost examples, or deployment example endpoints. No code sends data to those URLs from the skill package.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is limited to Markdown links and sample commands. I found no request logic, webhook, or exfiltration behavior.

low_findings.title: False positive: Placeholder environment and secret names
low_findings.description: Environment variable and secret detections are placeholder examples used to teach skill authors about configuration. No real credential value is present in the reviewed evidence.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The values are names such as INTERNAL_API_KEY, SECRET_KEY, and APP_DATABASE_URL in illustrative documentation. No literal secret or runtime access path was found.

low_findings.title: False positive: Weak crypto and vulnerability examples
low_findings.description: Weak cryptography and security issue detections appear in example skill content, review examples, or frontmatter text. They are used as educational samples rather than executable implementation code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are presented as examples, including bad and corrected patterns. They do not define runnable package code.

low_findings.title: Low residual risk: Skill can encourage command-oriented documentation
low_findings.description: The skill recommends including executable commands and scripts in generated skills. This is legitimate for workflow documentation, but users should review generated commands before running them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill intentionally guides authors to document commands and validation steps. This is a real but limited risk because the package itself does not execute them.

low_findings.title: False positive: Obfuscation heuristic on Markdown content
low_findings.description: The high-entropy and dangerous-combination heuristic is caused by mixed Markdown, Chinese text, code fences, URLs, and placeholder credential names. Manual review found readable documentation, not encoded payloads or hidden execution logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited files are plain Markdown documentation and examples. The risky combination is aggregated from examples, not from a runnable implementation path.

content.value_statement: Writing effective AI skills is hard when structure, triggers, and examples are unclear. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a practical guide for drafting and reviewing SKILL.md files.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to write SKILL.md frontmatter with clear names and trigger descriptions.
Provides simple and workflow skill templates for new skill drafts.
Shows good and bad examples of skill structure, descriptions, workflows, and outputs.
Gives checklists for naming, content completeness, structure, scripts, and final review.
Guides when to keep content in SKILL.md and when to split references into extra files.
limitations: Does not automatically validate a skill package against a marketplace policy.
Does not run generated commands, scripts, tests, or deployment workflows by itself.
Uses examples that may need adaptation for each project and tool environment.
Focuses on skill writing quality, not full security review of generated skills.

use_cases.title: Draft a New Team Skill
use_cases.description: Create a clear SKILL.md for a repeated team workflow with triggers, steps, examples, and success checks.

use_cases.title: Improve an Existing Skill
use_cases.description: Review an existing skill for vague descriptions, weak examples, missing workflow steps, and unclear activation rules.

use_cases.title: Standardize Skill Templates
use_cases.description: Use the included templates and checklists to make new skills follow a consistent documentation pattern.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Basic Skill
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a new simple skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a basic SKILL.md for a skill named [skill-name]. Include frontmatter, when to use it, steps, examples, and a checklist.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Skill Activation
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this SKILL.md description and When to Use section. Make the trigger keywords specific, concise, and easy for Claude to match.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Workflow Skill
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a multi-step process
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn this workflow into a SKILL.md with preparation steps, decision points, validation checks, error handling, and two output examples.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Skill Quality
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this skill against naming, description, structure, example, script, and final review checklists. Return prioritized fixes.

output_examples.input: Create a skill for generating API documentation from FastAPI routes.
output_examples.output: A complete skill outline with frontmatter, activation triggers, a documentation workflow, validation steps, and expected user-facing results.

output_examples.input: Review this skill because it does not activate reliably.
output_examples.output: A concise review that identifies vague trigger language, missing keywords, weak examples, and concrete description improvements.

output_examples.input: Convert our deployment runbook into a workflow skill.
output_examples.output: A workflow-focused skill draft with preparation checks, ordered phases, failure handling, rollback guidance, and success criteria.

best_practices: Keep the description specific, third-person, and tied to user phrases that should trigger the skill.
Include realistic examples with inputs, expected outputs, validation steps, and failure handling.
Split long reference material into nearby files and keep SKILL.md focused on core workflow guidance.
anti_patterns: Do not use vague names such as helper, utility, or manager without a specific task domain.
Do not fill the skill with general knowledge that the model already knows.
Do not include commands or scripts without clear purpose, safety checks, and expected results.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create SKILL.md files with clear metadata, activation rules, workflows, examples, and quality checks.

faq.question: Can it create both simple and workflow skills?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for basic skills and more complex multi-step workflow skills.

faq.question: Does it execute commands from the templates?
faq.answer: No. The command blocks are examples for skill authors and require separate user review before use.

faq.question: Is the content only for Claude?
faq.answer: No. The guidance can help Claude, Codex, and Claude Code users write clearer skill instructions.

faq.question: How should long skills be organized?
faq.answer: Keep core instructions in SKILL.md and move detailed references, examples, or scripts into nearby files.

faq.question: Does it replace a security audit?
faq.answer: No. It improves skill writing quality but does not replace security review for scripts, tools, or external access.
